response.justification: The candidate is a skilled full-stack and backend engineer with a strong track record in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, but does not meet the mandatory requirements for this role. C++ experience is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments and a university thesis project—far short of the required 5–10 years. There is no evidence of Windows-platform C++ development, no SQL Server or Oracle experience, and no meaningful capital markets or fixed income domain knowledge. The RIDE Capital stint involves fintech tooling in TypeScript/GraphQL and does not constitute investment banking or capital markets systems work.
strengths: Broad engineering range with demonstrated ability to learn and ship across many languages and platforms
Strong software craftsmanship discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and pairing applied consistently
Some financial-services adjacent exposure through RIDE Capital and investment-platform work
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months professionally at NI); C++ is not even listed in the candidate's own skills section
No Windows-platform development history; no SQL Server or Oracle database experience
Zero fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge
